title: Aureline approval-ticket lifecycle contract
description: Boundary schema for one approval_ticket_record frozen in docs/trust/approval_ticket_lifecycle_contract.md. Approval tickets are inspectable authority objects that bind actor identity, user-confirmed intent, action family, target identity, sandbox or capability envelope, policy epoch, and expiry to high-risk local mutations, remote attach operations, credential projections, provider mutations, and policy/admin changes. Tickets are designed to be export-safe by default: raw secret bytes, raw provider tokens, raw delegated-token bodies, raw URLs, raw file bodies, and raw policy payloads MUST NOT appear on the ticket; export surfaces rely on stable opaque ids plus an intent_digest and optional preview_hash to preserve lineage without leaking raw bodies. Only the shell, the policy service, and the supervisor MAY mint tickets; AI flows, extensions, recipes, CLI scripts, browser companions, and remote helpers MAY request approval but MAY NOT grant themselves authority. Adding a new action_class, issuer_class, actor_class, use_posture, high_risk_flag, revoke_reason, or redaction_class value is additive-minor and bumps approval_ticket_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

approval_ticket_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the approval-ticket lifecycle boundary.

opaque_id.description: Opaque id safe for logs, traces, support bundles, CLI output, evidence packets, and exports. Raw secret material, raw URLs, raw file paths, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

hex_hash.description: Hash-algorithm-prefixed digest (for example 'sha256:7b9f0b2d...') computed over structured fields (intent descriptor, preview packet, etc). Raw bodies never cross the boundary.

reviewable_sentence.description: Reviewable sentence safe for UI, CLI, docs/help, support export, and audit evidence. Do not include raw secrets, raw URLs, raw policy payloads, raw file bodies, or raw provider payloads.

issuer_class.description: Only the shell, the policy service, and the supervisor MAY mint approval tickets. Any other issuer is forged and denied.

provider_actor_class.description: Actor class the ticket is spent as. unknown_actor_class is non-spendable; a surface that cannot resolve the actor routes to a repair hook rather than issuing a ticket under an ambiguous connected state.

action_class.description: Frozen action-class vocabulary. Every ticket names exactly one action class. A ticket admits the named class and nothing broader; widening mints a new ticket with fresh lineage.

trust_state.description: Workspace trust posture at ticket issue.

use_posture.description: Frozen use-posture vocabulary. single_use is the default; bounded_reuse requires an explicit counter; session_scoped is forbidden for release_publish and trust_or_policy_admin_change.

bounded_reuse_counter.description: Bounded-reuse counter. Present only when use_posture is bounded_reuse.

high_risk_flag.description: Frozen high-risk flag vocabulary. Every flag has approval-time and spend-time gating rules; a flag without its rule is denied at issue.

revoke_reason.description: Typed revocation reason. Revocation is immediate and denies further use.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class shared across authority, approval, evidence, support, and audit packets.

human_summary.description: Human-legible summary the user confirmed at approval time.

machine_descriptor.description: Machine-readable command/mutation/projection descriptor. Shape is action-class-specific. MUST NOT contain raw secret bytes, raw URLs, raw delegated-token bodies, raw file bodies, or raw policy payloads; those cross boundaries as opaque refs only.

audit_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to approval_event_record instances that describe lifecycle transitions.

support_export_refs.description: Opaque refs to support/export packets that include this ticket's lineage.

evidence_packet_refs.description: Opaque refs to evidence packets (for example, release publish, signing evidence, incident evidence).

mutation_journal_refs.description: Opaque refs to mutation-journal entries that cite this ticket.

incident_refs.description: Opaque refs to incident workspaces or repair transactions that reference this ticket.

evidence_refs.description: Export-safe evidence and reconstruction refs. The refs are the truth; consumers MUST NOT infer lineage from freeform prose alone.

ticket_schema_version.description: Pinned schema version embedded into the ticket so a replayed ticket is validated against the exact shape it was minted under.

actor_subject.description: Acting identity (user id, install id, delegated binding id, project-scoped grant id, or policy-injected service id).

issuing_surface.description: Where the ticket was issued from (shell prompt id, policy-decision id, supervisor path).

intent_digest.description: Export-safe digest of the canonicalized intent descriptor the user approved. Consumers join on intent_digest (not raw machine_descriptor) across support exports, audit packets, and replay checks.

command_family_or_action_ref.description: Stable reference to the command family or provider action the ticket admits.

workspace_or_workset_scope_ref.description: Workspace/workset scope ref the ticket applies inside.

target_identity_ref.description: Execution target / provider target / credential handle / policy subject the ticket is bound to. Export-safe opaque ref (no raw paths or URLs).

sandbox_profile_or_capability_hash.description: Capability envelope the admitted action runs under.

projection_mode_ref.description: Projection-mode ref when action_class is credential_projection; empty string otherwise.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Browser-handoff packet ref when action_class is browser_handoff; empty string otherwise.

execution_context_id.description: Execution-context id the ticket runs inside.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch at issue. Presenting a ticket at a later policy epoch is re-evaluated; silent continued-use is forbidden.

use_count.description: Observed number of successful spends of this ticket id. For bounded_reuse, equals bounded_reuse_counter.used_count. For single_use, must be 0 or 1.

high_risk_flags.description: Set of high-risk flags carried by this ticket.

step_up_required_flag.description: When true, the ticket is not spendable until a fresh authenticator event clears it.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Optional rollback-checkpoint ref the ticket commits to restoring on denial or failure. Required when high_risk_flags contains destructive_local_action.

preview_hash.description: Export-safe preview hash over structured preview fields (not raw bodies). Required for destructive_local_action and for action classes with irreversible effect.

preview_ref.description: Optional local preview-record ref the user approved against. Local-only pointer; exports prefer preview_hash.

ticket_lineage.description: Ordered list of predecessor ticket ids when this ticket derives authority from another ticket (deferred publish drain, delegated credential spend, etc).

revoke_epoch.description: Null until revoked; populated on revocation. Paired with revoke_reason.

revoke_reason.description: Typed revoke reason. Non-null when revoke_epoch is non-null.

allOf.description: projection_mode_ref is required when action_class is credential_projection.
browser_handoff_packet_ref is required when action_class is browser_handoff.
bounded_reuse_counter MUST be present when use_posture is bounded_reuse, and MUST be absent otherwise.
session_scoped use_posture is forbidden for release_publish and trust_or_policy_admin_change.
revoke_reason MUST be present when revoke_epoch is non-null.
rollback_checkpoint_ref is required when high_risk_flags contains destructive_local_action.
preview_hash is required for action classes with irreversible effect.
